DEPUTY CONFIRMED THAT TORNADO WAS ON GROUND AROUND 1729 CDT. ANOTHER CALLER SAW TORNADO RIP ROOF OFF BARN WHILE MOVING EAST. SEVERAL OTHER PUBLIC REPORTS OF A TORNADO WERE RECEIVED. NO REPORTS OF INJURIES.

SHAVE REPORT. LATE REPORT FROM 6-7-11.

OBSERVER REPORTED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN HIS FARM FIELDS. IT WAS A BRIEF TOUCH DOWN...NO MORE THAN A FEW MINUTES. THERE HAVE BEEN OTHER REPORTS OF ROTATION OF THE WALL CLOUD IN ELGIN AND BENTLY. NO DAMAGE REPORTS.
STILL HAILING.
STILL GETTING PEA SIZE HAIL.

1 FOOT OF WATER OVER WEST AND EAST BOUND LANES OF INTERSTATE 94 MILE MARKER 106... RELATED 3 CAR ACCIDENT WITH HEAVY RAIN AND HAIL.
INTERSTATE 94 UNDERPASS AT MILE MARKER 103 NEAR EXIT 102... RELAYED FROM STATE RADIO...

EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 8 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF ELGIN AROUND 830 PM MDT AND MOVED NORTH-NORTHEAST. IT LIFTED 3 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHEAST OF ELGIN...AT AROUND 845 PM MDT. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 15 MINUTES...AND COVERED A PATH LENGTH OF AROUND 6 MILES. THE TORNADO WAS NEARLY A HALF MILE WIDE AT ITS MAXIMUM WIDTH. NO FEWER THAN 4 FARMSTEADS WERE DAMAGED ALONG THE PATH. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS AROUND 6 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF ELGIN. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS WERE AROUND 120 MPH.
REPORTED BY COUNTY SHERIFF
REPORTED BY COUNTY SHERIFF

TWO OUTBUILDINGS HAD ROOFS BLOWN OFF. ONE OF THEM HAD WALLS COLLAPSED...BUT HAD DOORS OPEN ON SOUTH SIDE WHEN STORM HIT. ALL DEBRIS WAS BLOWN TO NORTHEAST. DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG A LINE FROM 4 MILES EAST OF NEW SALEM TO 9 MILES EAST OF NEW SALEM.
RELAY BY STATE RADIO
PUBLIC REPORT STILL ON GROUND AT 230 PM CDT. COULD NO LONGER SEE THE TORNADO VIEW WAS OBSTRUCTED BY RAIN.

LASTED UNTIL 205 PM. PEAK WIND SPEEDS AROUND 40 MPH. THIS IS FALCONER ESTATES SUBDIVISION. SEVERAL HOMES HAD ROOF AND GUTTER DAMAGE. LEAVES...SMALL BRANCHES...AND FRUIT WERE KNOCKED OFF OF TREES. VEGETABLE GARDENS WERE DESTROYED.

DOUBLE POLED WOOD TRANSMISSION LINES SNAPPED IN HALF. HEAVY RANCH SIGN WITH THREE FOOT CONCRETE ANCHORS RIPPED OUT OF THE GROUND. CAMPER FLIPPED OFF OF THE HIGHWAY AND OVER TREE ROW 25 YARDS. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH.
MINOR DAMAGE TO FARMSTEAD. SHINGLES BLOWN OFF OF TRAILER HOME AND SMALL BUILDING BLOWN OVER. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.

EXTENSIVE TREE BRANCH DAMAGE IN HAZELTON. THREE INCH TO SIX INCH IN DIAMETER BRANCHES BLOWN OFF OF TREES. GAS STATION ON THE WEST SIDE OF TOWN HAD DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OVER THE GAS PUMPS. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH.
ALSO HAD 1 INCH HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN.
EMPTY GRAIN BINS DAMAGED. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH.
HEAVY RAIN.
18 THOUSAND BUSHEL GRAIN BIN MOVED 250 YDS. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE WITH DOWNED TREES POINTING IN RANDOM DIRECTIONS. TREES 60 YEARS OLD...100 FT TALL.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. 1800 FEET OF SHELTER BELT MISSING. GRAIN BIN DESTROYED AND THROWN INTO TREE IN FRONT YARD. NWS DAMAGE SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED.
GRAIN BIN WITH ESTIMATED WEIGHT OF 17 TO 20 THOUSAND POUNDS...10 TONS...WAS LIFTED AND ROLLED NEARLY THREE QUARTERS OF A MILE FROM ITS ORIGINAL LOCATION TO ANOTHER FARMSTAD.
TREES UPROOTED...SOME AS LARGE AS 4 FEET IN DIAMETER AND GREATER THAN 100 FEET TALL.

DELAYED REPORT. DAMAGE ASSESSMENT TEAM SURVEYED DAMAGE AT A FARMSTEAD 5 MILES NORTHWEST OF MERRICOURT. RESIDENTS WITNESSED A TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN A SHELTERBELT AS THEY TOOK COVER IN THEIR HOME. A POLE BARN WAS DAMAGED. NUMEROUS TREE TRUNKS AND LIMBS WERE SNAPPED AND BLOWN IN DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS. THE TREE DAMAGE INDICATE WIND SPEEDS OF AROUND 90 MPH...OR LOW EF1 DAMAGE. TORNADO LASTED 2 MINUTES. PATH LENGTH...100 YARDS. PATH WIDTH...25 YARDS.
